- Challenge overview (June) https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-cap-blog-posts/june-developer-challenge-cap-knowledge/ba-p/14405157 

Did You Know? #18 – Sharing and synchronising profiles
- ABAP Cleaner https://github.com/SAP/abap-cleaner

Chapter titles

00:00 Intro
00:07 CodeJam report live from Mexico with Tom and Rich
02:00 Building an LLM Agent using CAP
03:20 Sapphire 2026 highlights for SAP Business Data Cloud
05:21 Developer Challenges May into June
07:00 DYK #18 Sharing and synchronising profiles

Transcript

[Tom & Rich]
Hey, everybody. We're coming to you from a Code Jam Roadshow in Mexico. We started the week in Mexico City, Mexico. Rich, you had the first day. What were you teaching? Yeah, so we did ABAP Cloud and Joule, and we had a great day. We went through the exercises and saw how Joule can assist us in creating RAP applications quickly and easily. and everybody seemed to have a good time yeah and then I led day two which was the code-based agents code jam where we were looking at creating agents from scratch also how agents help us with coding activities we gave a little look at the new Joule Studio but also solved a crime who stole our painting from the gallery using multiple agents working together. So we get to see how different personas can be applied to agents, how different levels of knowledge and expertise can be applied to agents and how then they work together in a crew to solve a problem. Fun stuff. Yeah. And here we are Wednesday. It was a travel day to Monterey. I think that's where we are. That's all right. You see the beautiful mountains behind us. outside the city. And the moon, right? Yeah, and a crane, evidently. But now we're going to do it again. So all over again tomorrow, we're doing another code jam here in Monterey. Is it at the SAP offices? Tomorrow's at the SAP offices. Again, we're doing all ABAP cloud and Joule. Some of our friends are coming from Mexico City as well. And yeah, so we'll have a good time again. All right. Yeah. So until next time, maybe we'll be coming to a city near you in the near future check out all the new stuff that we have yep bye for now.

[Ajay]
Hello everyone agentic systems have been gaining significant traction lately and justifiably so they enable the development of ai systems capable of iteratively reasoning and executing toward the goal-oriented solutions while numerous frameworks and libraries exist for building standalone agents The SAP Cloud Application Programming Model now presents a distinct opportunity to architect agents that encapsulate domain -specific knowledge and seamlessly integrate with SAP ecosystem, including its native services and backend systems. We have a tutorial blog post by Johannes Vogt from CAP Enablement Team, where he walks us through the implementation of a lightweight LLM -powered agent leveraging the CAP framework. CAP integrates nicely with identity workflow and allows us to easily expose our services as tools for the agent, while also ensuring that all the necessary service level logic and permissions are applied. Since production-like qualities like authentication, authorization, and database transactions are handled by CAP, we can focus on the domain. There are further links to the blog post that you can explore for advanced topic and use cases. So give it a try today and begin your agentic journey using SAP Cloud Application Programming Model. Links to the blog post will be in the description below.

[Witalij]
If you are interested in all things data but missed Sapphire, or you just want the short version without sitting through hours and hours of sessions, our product expert at SAP, KP Sauer, has you covered. He recorded a few-minute recap of top new features in SAP Business Data Cloud to help you quickly catch up on the most important announcements. Here are some of the key topics he covered in his video. The announcement of the SAP Business AI Platform and what actually changed from an architectural perspective. Why SAP HANA Cloud is becoming a core component of the Business Data Cloud strategy. How Reltio and SAP Master Data Governance fit together inside BDC. And finally, Joule agents operating directly within the business data fabric. So, if you want the high -level takeaways without the noise, this is definitely You'll find the link in the description. Plus, this week, the Mastering SAP Data Architecture course officially went live on learning.sap.com, alongside the launch of the new SAP Certified Data Architect Certification. This is not a generic introduction
course. It is designed for data practitioners and data architects who need to operate on architectural depth and understand how enterprise data strategy is evolving in the AI era. The course covers topics like architecting data strategy for the agentic AI era, applying established frameworks including TOGAF, DAMA DMBOK and the SAP enterprise architecture framework in real-world scenarios and exploring concepts like data mesh, semantic layers, and knowledge graphs for enterprise AI. So if you are working in the enterprise data architecture, analytics, governance, or AI strategy, this is definitely worth your time as well.

[DJ]
The developer challenge for the month of May is coming to an end this week. Antonio did a great job accompanying and guiding us on a journey to learn about event driven architectures and AI. In week one, we acquainted ourselves with events. In week two, we connected to the broker and started to consume those events. In week three, we looked at vectorizing the event payload. And in this final week, week four, we're looking at storing vectors and enabling retrieval augmented generation. That's RAG. While this month's challenge is still active, June is just around the corner. So we wanted to give you a heads up that the next challenge is ready and will start on Monday. June's challenge encourages you to dig into the wonders and delights of the CAP documentation, aka CAPIRE, to unearth truth and beauty in this exemplary and valuable resource. Why? Because today, more than ever, we as developers need to ensure we retain knowledge and double down on our understanding of architectures, toolkits, frameworks, and perhaps most of all, of best practices. More on that in the challenge intro post, which is published now. When the challenge starts, you can also head back to this challenge intro post to find your way to the tasks. Good luck, have fun, and as always, happy learning.

[Sheena]
Did you know that you can maintain a synchronized ABAP profile across your team by creating and sharing a team profile in ABAP Cleaner? Usually, ABAP Cleaner profiles are created locally based on the required rules. Let us consider a scenario where a project team would like to come up with a synchronized profile that can be used by everyone in the team. Here, the lead developer or architect can formulate a profile where all the required rules are activated and this profile is then exported using the export button to a synchronized folder, which may be the team's OneDrive or SharePoint. Team members can then use this profile by clicking on the folders button to add the synchronized folder as an additional read-only source of team profiles. In case of any further changes, we can simply export it again to override the synchronized folder and thereby sharing the updates to everyone, provided it's not the default profile.

entry.title: Re: Not able to add a new Routine Number inside the Prici...
entry.content:
Good day @Ramkinker 

Thank you for your question.

Based on your screenshot and the requirement, the key point is that the old on-premise approach of copying a standard VOFM/R program into a Z routine is not available in the cloud system. In the Public Cloud, pricing routine extensions are only possible through the released pricing BAdIs in the Custom Fields app, and only for the supported pricing use cases such as condition amount, condition base value and pricing requirements. See KBA 2885513 and 2756560 for more insights.

In your case, the BAdIs you already checked are the correct released ones, but they do not provide a changing parameter to replace or overwrite the material group used by the standard routine. This means you cannot create a new routine number in the same way you did in on-premise and use it to change the default material group logic inside the standard pricing routine.

So, in simple terms the following apply:

  • You cannot copy a standard pricing routine into a Z routine like in on premise.
  • You cannot directly change the standard material group determination used by SAP standard pricing routine logic.
  • The pricing BAdIs listed can influence condition amount, condition base value, or whether a condition should be considered but they do not allow you to change the standard material group field used internally by the pricing routine.
  • If your requirement is only to include or exclude a condition based on another material group related value, then the Public Cloud offers you the possible approach of using pricing requirement BAdI.
  • If the requirement is to determine prices or discounts using another material grouping field, the better design is to model this through pricing condition tables, access sequences, custom fields for pricing, or condition records instead of trying to change the standard routine.

There is also extensibility possibility for pricing condition records using custom fields. This means, if the field you need is not the standard material group but a different product attribute, you can evaluate whether it can be added as a custom field for pricing and then used in condition records.

Basically, this means you have two realistic options:

Option 1 (cleanest approach) is for you to use pricing configuration instead of a new routine. If the requirement is to price based on another grouping logic, create or adjust the pricing configuration using:

  • Condition table
  • Access sequence
  • Condition type
  • Pricing procedure
  • Condition records

Option 2 if for you to use a pricing requirement BAdI only to control whether the condition is active. If the requirement is not to change the material group itself but only to decide whether the pricing condition should apply or not, then use:

  • PRICING_DOCUMENT_REQUIREMENT
  • PRCG_DOCUMENT_ITEM_REQUIREMENT

These can be used to return whether a pricing condition should be considered. However, they should not be expected to change the standard material group or the standard access field used for pricing. Note that the logic is used to determine whether a condition is considered during pricing.

For your exact requirement, if you need a new routine number that changes the standard routine’s material group logic, then this is not supported in the cloud system with the released extensibility options.

The best direction is therefore:

  1. Check whether the “other Material Group” can be represented as a standard pricing field, product hierarchy, product attribute or a custom field enabled for pricing.
  2. If yes, create a custom condition table and access sequence using that field.
  3. Assign the access sequence to the relevant condition type.
  4. Maintain the condition records in Manage Price-Sales.
  5. Test the pricing result in a sales order
  6. If the field cannot be made available for pricing and cannot be handled through the released BAdIs, you may consider raising an influence request.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Embracing the Future at Sapphire 2026 - A Journey with Migration and Modernization Assistants
entry.content:
Welcome to Sapphire 2026, where the future meets enterprise transformation! This year, attendees had the privilege of diving into the world of Migration and Modernization Assistants, a vision brought to life by industry leaders Soeren Ruder and Sonja Liénard during a riveting session at the conference.

Reflecting on the Journey

The session kicked off with Soeren, CPO at Cloud ALM, drawing us into the transformational journey that began back in 2021 with the launch of RISE with SAP. Although initially branded as "Business Transformation as a Service," Soeren was candid about the lack of substantial transformation at the onset. However, fast forward to today, and we see a company that has made significant strides with impressive acquisitions like Signavio, LeanIX, and WalkMe, reiterating their commitment to authentic business transformation.

Recognizing that transformation is not without its complexities and costs, Soeren emphasized the role of the latest AI capabilities and emerging technologies in addressing these challenges, marking 2026 as a significant milestone in embedding migration and modernization into the core of business transformation motions.

Introducing the Magnificent Seven: Migration and Modernization Assistants

Soeren's presentation took us through the innovative toolchain designed to minimize risks and costs associated with migration projects. Here’s a glimpse into these groundbreaking assistants:

  1. System Analysis Assistant: The cornerstone of transformation projects, this tool provides insights into system data, custom code, and overall requirements, laying the groundwork for seamless migration.
  2. Data Management Assistant: Tackling data inconsistencies and offering actionable solutions to expedite the migration process, this assistant leverages AI for rapid and effective data cleanup.
  3. Custom Code Assistant: With Joule for Developer, this assistant not only ensures code compatibility with SAP S/4HANA but also dives deep into semantic understanding, offering a path from the complexity of custom code to optimized standard processes.
  4. Configuration Assistant: Automating system configuration by smartly combining best practice models with user-defined scopes, this assistant sets up systems efficiently.
  5. Test Management Assistant: Redefining test case creation with AI, this assistant collaborates with Tricentis to reduce manual effort, accelerating testing phases with precision and automation.
  6. Rollout Assistant: Simplifying regional expansions, this tool enables parallel rollouts by ensuring template compatibility and adaptations.
  7. Project Management Assistant: Moving beyond traditional project oversight methods, this tool makes project management dynamic and responsive, integrating intelligent feedback with a single source of truth for progress tracking.

Live Demonstration: A Real-Time Experience

The session transitioned to an enthralling demo led by Sonja, where attendees witnessed the synergy of these assistants within a unified platform. The demo illustrated processes across three different roles - project manager, custom code lead and developer - demonstrating how these assistants streamline tasks and optimize project execution.

Through strategic task delegation and AI-powered automated code remediation, the demo revealed a future where ERP migration is transformed from labor-intensive endeavors to efficient, outcome-driven processes.

Commitment to Excellence: SAP’s Vision Realized

The announcement of Siemens as an early adopter resonates with SAP’s confidence in these Migration and Modernization Assistants. Siemens’ alignment with SAP's goals of reducing migration efforts is a testament to the transformative power these tools hold in real-world applications.

Explore More at Sapphire 2026

With numerous sessions exploring the depths of data management, custom code optimization, and task automation, Sapphire 2026 promises a wealth of knowledge and innovation. Attendees are encouraged to visit the Agent Led Tool Chain booth for deeper insights and discussions with experts.

As we conclude this session, it's evident that the Migration and Modernization Assistants are not merely tools—they are harbingers of a new era in enterprise transformation. Enjoy the Sapphire experience, and here's to shaping the future of digital business transformation!

Thank you for joining us at Sapphire 2026.

entry.title: Re: find sql anywhere development files
entry.content:
The point is that you seem to be using some sort of make tool. The environment variable is SQLANY17 (don't know where you got the braces from). Many years ago, the make tool we used would have expected $(SQLANY17). I am suggesting to you that you use the internet to find out how to reference an environment variable using whatever make tool you employ. Here is a piece of an ancient makefile that we used 30 years ago.

!ifndef H_DIR                                          
!   define H_DIR $(DBDIR)\h                            
!endif                                                 
!ifndef WSQLH_DIR                                      
!   define WSQLH_DIR $(H_DIR)\wsqlh                    
!endif                                                 
!ifndef NEWDBDIR                                       
!   define NEWDBDIR $(DBDIR)\newstore                  
!endif                                                 
!ifndef HVER_DIR_ROOT                                  
!   define HVER_DIR_ROOT    h\h$(WSQLVER)              
!endif                                                 
!define HVER_DIR            $(DBDIR)\$(HVER_DIR_ROOT)

entry.title: Ledger not active in Chart of Depreciation & FYV mismatch (Copied Chart of Dep. on Practice Server)
entry.content:
hello SAP Experts,

I am currently setting up a configuration on a practice server and am hitting a roadblock while trying to assign/register a new Company Code (GM01) to a Chart of Depreciation.

For context, I copied the Chart of Depreciation (GM01) from another country's standard template (such as US / India) into my current setup. When saving, I am receiving the following two hard errors:

  1. "Fiscal year variants in company code GM01 have differing start dates"

  2. "Ledger Q1 defined in CoCd GM01 is not active in chart of depreciation GM01"

What I have tried so far:

  • I tried creating a new ledger and activating it with the Company Code again, but the system continues to state that the specific ledger is not active.

Since this is a practice environment and the Chart of Depreciation was copied across different country standards, I suspect there is a fundamental mismatch in the underlying calendar/fiscal settings and ledger mappings.

Could anyone please advise on how to resolve this? Specifically:

  1. How can I align the differing start dates for the Fiscal Year Variants caused by this copy?

  2. What are the exact configuration steps to successfully activate/map Ledger Q1 within this copied Chart of Depreciation GM01?

Any step-by-step guidance or advice on how to clean up these copied settings would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your help!

Best regards,
